zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Ince於大約1328年出生在英國藍卡爾特拉福德 曼諾時，她的父親Robert Ince26歲，母親Margery de Trafford23歲。她於1350年9月29日與Sir Henry de Trafford Kt在英國英格蘭爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1416年1月17日在她的家鄉享年89歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Ince于大约1328年出生在英国藍卡爾特拉福德 曼諾时，她的父亲Robert Ince26岁，母亲Margery de Trafford23岁。她于1350年9月29日与Sir Henry de Trafford Kt在英国英格兰爾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1416年1月17日在她的家乡享年89岁。

nameMeanings.name: Ince
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from Ince in Makerfield (Lancashire), Ince (Cheshire), or Ince Blundell (Lancashire). The placenames derive from Proto-Welsh ïnïs ‘island’.

possibly a post-medieval West Midlands variant of Hints, a habitational name from either of two places called Hints (Staffordshire, Shropshire). The placenames probably derive from Proto-Welsh hïnt ‘way, path’ + Middle English plural -es.

possibly a variant with plural or post-medieval excrescent -s of Inn, from residence at an inn or inns, from Middle English in (singular) or innes (plural) ‘tavern, lodging(s), tenement(s)’. There seems also to have been some confusion with Scottish Innes .

variants.name: Ence
meanings: Americanized form of South German   Enz  , a short form of   Anselm  .
History: Two brothers, Johannes and Gottlieb Enz, immigrated from northern Switzerland to southern Utah around 1860. Their descendants bear the surname Ence.

variants.name: Innes
meanings: Scottish: shortened form of McInnis , from Gaelic Mag Aonghuis ‘son of Aonghus’.
Scottish: habitational name from the barony of Innes in Urquhart (Morayshire), named from Gaelic inis ‘island’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Ince was born about 1328, in Trafford Manor, Lancashire, England, her father, Robert Ince, was 26 and her mother, Margery de Trafford, was 23. She married Sir Henry de Trafford Kt on 29 September 1350, in Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 4 daughters. She died on 17 January 1416, in her hometown, at the age of 89.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Ince est née environ 1328, à Trafford Manor, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, Robert Ince, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Margery de Trafford, avait 23 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Henry de Trafford Kt le 29 septembre 1350, à Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée le 17 janvier 1416, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 89 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Ince(이)가 대략 1328년 영국 랭카실르 트래퍼드 먼어에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert Ince(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margery de Trafford(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 1350년 9월 29일 영국 잉글랜드 체실르에서 Sir Henry de Trafford Kt(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1416년 1월 17일 그녀의 고향에서 89세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.Smithills, Bolton, Lancashire, England: смитиллс холл чапел, болтон, ланкашир, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Ince родилась около 1328 г. в местности траффорд манор, ланкашир, Англия, её отцу по имени Robert Ince было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Margery de Trafford, 23. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Henry de Trafford Kt 29 сентября 1350 г. в местности чешир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла 17 января 1416 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 89 лет.
